zsh.c(3) Library Functions Manual zsh.c(3)

NAME

zsh.c - Zsh shell completion script generator with category grouping.

SYNOPSIS

Functions


asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE *output)

Detailed Description

Zsh shell completion script generator with category grouping.

Definition in file zsh.c.

Function Documentation

asciichat_error_t completions_generate_zsh (FILE * output)

Definition at line 197 of file zsh.c.

197                                                          {
198   if (!output) {
199     return SET_ERRNO(ERROR_INVALID_PARAM, "Output stream cannot be NULL");
200   }
201 
202   /* Load binary-level and discovery options for binary-level completion.
203    * Binary-level completion (options before mode name like --color-mode) uses
204    * _arguments with -n -S flags to establish proper zsh completion context.
205    * Without _arguments, completion functions fail with "can only be called from
206    * completion function" error, causing zsh to display "corrections (errors: N)"
207    * messages. The -n flag disables defaults and -S disables short option
208    * processing, allowing safe context establishment. */
209   size_t binary_count = 0;
210   const option_descriptor_t *binary_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, true, &binary_count);
211 
212   size_t discovery_count = 0;
213   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY, false, &discovery_count);
214 
215   fprintf(output, "compdef _ascii_chat ascii-chat\n"
216                   "# Zsh completion script for ascii-chat\n"
217                   "# Generated from options registry - DO NOT EDIT MANUALLY\n"
218                   "\n"
219                   "# Enable approximate completion for ascii-chat - suggests close matches\n"
220                   "# when the typed string doesn't match exactly (e.g., --colr-mode -> --color-mode)\n"
221                   "zstyle ':completion:*:ascii-chat:*' completer _complete _approximate\n"
222                   "zstyle ':completion:*:ascii-chat:*:approximate:*' max-errors 2 numeric\n"
223                   "\n"
224                   "# Device index completion helpers\n"
225                   "_ascii_chat_webcam_indices() {\n"
226                   "  local -a indices\n"
227                   "  local output\n"
228                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-webcams 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
229                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
230                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
231                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
232                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
233                   "  _describe 'webcam' indices\n"
234                   "}\n"
235                   "\n"
236                   "_ascii_chat_microphone_indices() {\n"
237                   "  local -a indices\n"
238                   "  local output\n"
239                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-microphones 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
240                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
241                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
242                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
243                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
244                   "  _describe 'microphone' indices\n"
245                   "}\n"
246                   "\n"
247                   "_ascii_chat_speakers_indices() {\n"
248                   "  local -a indices\n"
249                   "  local output\n"
250                   "  output=$(\"${words[1]}\" --list-speakers 2>/dev/null) || return\n"
251                   "  while IFS= read -r line; do\n"
252                   "    [[ \"$line\" =~ ^[[:space:]]*([0-9-]+)[[:space:]]+(.+)$ ]] && \\\n"
253                   "      indices+=(\"${match[1]}:${match[2]}\")\n"
254                   "  done <<< \"$output\"\n"
255                   "  _describe 'speakers' indices\n"
256                   "}\n"
257                   "\n"
258                   "_ascii_chat_binary() {\n"
259                   "  local -a binary_opts=(\n");
260 
261   /* Add discovery options to binary-level completion */
262   if (discovery_opts) {
263     for (size_t i = 0; i < discovery_count; i++) {
264       fprintf(output, "    '--%s:", discovery_opts[i].long_name);
265       zsh_escape_desc(output, discovery_opts[i].help_text);
266       fprintf(output, "'\n");
267     }
268   }
269 
270   fprintf(output, "  )\n"
271                   "  _values 'option' \"${binary_opts[@]}\"\n"
272                   "}\n"
273                   "\n"
274                   "_ascii_chat_binary_grouped() {\n");
275 
276   /* Write binary-level options in grouped format */
277   if (binary_opts) {
278     zsh_write_value_cases(output, binary_opts, binary_count);
279     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, binary_opts, binary_count, "binary");
280   }
281 
282   fprintf(output, "}\n"
283                   "\n"
284                   "_ascii_chat() {\n"
285                   "  if [[ ${words[2]} == -* ]]; then\n"
286                   "    # Binary-level options: show both binary and discovery mode options\n"
287                   "    # (since discovery is the default mode that runs at binary level)\n"
288                   "    _ascii_chat_binary_grouped\n"
289                   "    _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
290                   "    return\n"
291                   "  fi\n"
292                   "\n"
293                   "  # Mode routing: check words[2] to determine which mode function to call\n"
294                   "  case $words[2] in\n"
295                   "    # Server-like modes\n"
296                   "    server)\n"
297                   "      _ascii_chat_server\n"
298                   "      return\n"
299                   "      ;;\n"
300                   "    discovery-service)\n"
301                   "      _ascii_chat_discovery_service\n"
302                   "      return\n"
303                   "      ;;\n"
304                   "\n"
305                   "    # Client-like modes\n"
306                   "    client)\n"
307                   "      _ascii_chat_client\n"
308                   "      return\n"
309                   "      ;;\n"
310                   "    mirror)\n"
311                   "      _ascii_chat_mirror\n"
312                   "      return\n"
313                   "      ;;\n"
314                   "\n"
315                   "    # Default mode (discovery) or mode selection\n"
316                   "    *)\n"
317                   "      if [[ $CURRENT -eq 2 ]]; then\n"
318                   "        # Completing the mode name itself - show available modes\n"
319                   "        local -a server_modes client_modes\n"
320                   "        server_modes=(\n");
321 
322   /* Generate server-like modes from registry */
323   size_t mode_server_count = 0;
324   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_server_descs = get_modes_by_group("server-like", &mode_server_count);
325   if (mode_server_descs) {
326     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_server_count; i++) {
327       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_server_descs[i].name, mode_server_descs[i].description);
328     }
329     SAFE_FREE(mode_server_descs);
330   }
331 
332   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
333                   "        client_modes=(\n");
334 
335   /* Generate client-like modes from registry (exclude discovery since it's the default mode) */
336   size_t mode_client_count = 0;
337   const mode_descriptor_t *mode_client_descs = get_modes_by_group("client-like", &mode_client_count);
338   if (mode_client_descs) {
339     for (size_t i = 0; i < mode_client_count; i++) {
340       /* Skip discovery mode - it's the default when no mode is specified */
341       if (strcmp(mode_client_descs[i].name, "discovery") == 0) {
342         continue;
343       }
344       fprintf(output, "          '%s:%s'\n", mode_client_descs[i].name, mode_client_descs[i].description);
345     }
346     SAFE_FREE(mode_client_descs);
347   }
348 
349   fprintf(output, "        )\n"
350                   "        _describe -t server-modes 'server-like modes' server_modes\n"
351                   "        _describe -t client-modes 'client-like modes' client_modes\n"
352                   "      else\n"
353                   "        # Default mode (discovery) - complete its options\n"
354                   "        _ascii_chat_discovery\n"
355                   "      fi\n"
356                   "      ;;\n"
357                   "  esac\n"
358                   "}\n"
359                   "\n"
360                   "# Server-like modes: handle incoming connections and stream management\n"
361                   "_ascii_chat_server() {\n");
362 
363   /* Server options - grouped by category */
364   size_t server_count = 0;
365   const option_descriptor_t *server_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_SERVER, false, &server_count);
366 
367   if (server_opts) {
368     zsh_write_value_cases(output, server_opts, server_count);
369     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, server_opts, server_count, "server");
370     SAFE_FREE(server_opts);
371   }
372 
373   fprintf(output, "}\n"
374                   "\n"
375                   "_ascii_chat_discovery_service() {\n");
376 
377   /* Discovery-service options - grouped by category */
378   size_t discovery_svc_count = 0;
379   const option_descriptor_t *discovery_svc_opts =
380       options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_DISCOVERY_SERVICE, false, &discovery_svc_count);
381 
382   if (discovery_svc_opts) {
383     zsh_write_value_cases(output, discovery_svc_opts, discovery_svc_count);
384     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, discovery_svc_opts, discovery_svc_count, "discovery_service");
385     SAFE_FREE(discovery_svc_opts);
386   }
387 
388   fprintf(output, "}\n"
389                   "\n"
390                   "# Client-like modes: connect to servers or render local media\n"
391                   "_ascii_chat_client() {\n");
392 
393   /* Client options - grouped by category */
394   size_t client_count = 0;
395   const option_descriptor_t *client_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_CLIENT, false, &client_count);
396 
397   if (client_opts) {
398     zsh_write_value_cases(output, client_opts, client_count);
399     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, client_opts, client_count, "client");
400     SAFE_FREE(client_opts);
401   }
402 
403   fprintf(output, "}\n"
404                   "\n"
405                   "_ascii_chat_mirror() {\n");
406 
407   /* Mirror options - grouped by category */
408   size_t mirror_count = 0;
409   const option_descriptor_t *mirror_opts = options_registry_get_for_display(MODE_MIRROR, false, &mirror_count);
410 
411   if (mirror_opts) {
412     zsh_write_value_cases(output, mirror_opts, mirror_count);
413     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, mirror_opts, mirror_count, "mirror");
414     SAFE_FREE(mirror_opts);
415   }
416 
417   fprintf(output, "}\n"
418                   "\n"
419                   "# Discovery mode: find sessions via discovery service (default mode when no mode specified)\n"
420                   "_ascii_chat_discovery() {\n");
421 
422   /* Discovery options - grouped by category (reuse already-loaded options) */
423   if (discovery_opts) {
424     zsh_write_value_cases(output, discovery_opts, discovery_count);
425     zsh_write_options_grouped(output, discovery_opts, discovery_count, "discovery");
426   }
427 
428   fprintf(output, "}\n"
429                   "\n"
430                   "_ascii_chat \"$@\"\n");
431 
432   SAFE_FREE(binary_opts);
433   SAFE_FREE(discovery_opts);
434 
435   return ASCIICHAT_OK;
436 }




References get_modes_by_group(),
    options_registry_get_for_display(), SAFE_FREE, and
    SET_ERRNO.


Referenced by completions_generate_for_shell().








Author


Zachary Fogg <me@zfo.gg>







  

    Version 0.9.0
    ascii-chat
